codeReview.summary: The event detail page implementation is solid and meets all core requirements. The page correctly displays event information (title, date/time, venue, price, remaining quantity, description), handles sports events with team names/flags, concert events with artist info, and provides Purchase/For sale buttons with confirmation dialogs. The API endpoint is clean with proper 404 handling. The implementation follows established patterns from the product detail page. A few non-blocking issues were identified around code duplication with the product detail page and potential image domain configuration concerns, but nothing that blocks feature acceptance.

issues.description: The concert artist image uses next/image's Image component with remote URLs (e.g. picsum.photos), which is configured in next.config.ts. However, if seed data or future data uses other image domains (not picsum.photos or i.pravatar.cc), the Image component will fail at runtime. The product detail page uses a plain tag instead, avoiding this constraint. This inconsistency could cause runtime errors for concert events with non-whitelisted image URLs.

Significant code duplication with app/products/[id]/page.tsx - the confirmation dialogs (Purchase and For sale), loading/error states, back button header, action buttons bar, and fetch pattern are nearly identical. These could be extracted into shared components (e.g., ConfirmationDialog, DetailPageHeader, ActionButtons). Not blocking but increases maintenance burden.

The action buttons container uses 'pb-20' which pushes the buttons up to account for the bottom tab navigation. However, the buttons themselves are in a fixed-position div at bottom-0, so the pb-20 creates a large gap between the buttons and the bottom of the viewport. This matches the product detail page pattern so it's consistent, but it means there's redundant vertical space at the bottom.

No test coverage for concert event rendering (only football events are tested in component tests). The mock concert event data is defined but never used. A test for concert-specific rendering (artist name, artist image display) would improve coverage of the type-branching UI logic.
No test coverage for the error/not-found state (when API returns non-ok response). The component has an error path that shows 'Event not found' but this is not exercised in tests.

formatDate and formatTime functions use locale-dependent formatting (toLocaleDateString/toLocaleTimeString) which could produce different output in different environments (CI vs dev machine). The handoff shows the worker's manual verification saw 'Jun 16, 2026 • 02:00 AM' but the test asserts specific date text from mock data using the same locale functions, so tests are internally consistent. Just noting the fragility for CI environments.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The event detail page uses next/image's Image component for concert artist images, while the product detail page uses a plain tag for product images. This inconsistency in image rendering approach is not documented in AGENTS.md. Both approaches have trade-offs (next/image requires domain whitelisting, triggers lint warnings). A convention should be established.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/events/[id]/page.tsx:128 uses from next/image; app/products/[id]/page.tsx:91 uses plain . next.config.ts only whitelists picsum.photos and i.pravatar.cc domains.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The confirmation dialog pattern (Purchase/For sale success dialogs) is duplicated across product detail and event detail pages with identical markup. This is a growing pattern that could benefit from a shared ConfirmationDialog component. AGENTS.md mentions 'Mock purchase/sale: confirmation dialogs only' in architecture.md but doesn't specify a shared component convention.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/events/[id]/page.tsx:239-283 and app/products/[id]/page.tsx:130-173 contain nearly identical dialog markup.

summary: The event-detail-page feature passes review. The implementation correctly builds the event detail page at app/events/[id]/page.tsx with all required elements: event banner (gradient for sports, artist image for concerts), team names with flags for sports events, artist info for concerts, date/time/venue display, ticket price in $XXX.XX format, remaining quantity, description, and Purchase/For sale action buttons with confirmation dialogs. The GET /api/events/[id] endpoint is clean with proper error handling. Back navigation uses router.back() which correctly returns to the originating tab. Tests cover API endpoints for all three event types plus 404 handling, and component tests verify rendering, navigation, and dialog interactions. Non-blocking issues include: missing concert-specific component test coverage, code duplication with product detail page, and inconsistent use of next/image vs plain img tags across detail pages.
